title: Revere's Eyewitness Account
description: Paul Revere, pressed with a direct question about Dillworth's recent movements, admits he saw Dillworth speaking privately with a well-dressed stranger near the Custom House three days ago. The stranger passed Dillworth something small — Revere thought it was a business card at the time, but now, hearing your questions, he is not so sure. 'The man had the look of a clerk,' Revere says, 'but dressed too well for honest clerking.'
